name: Krupaj Spring
description: Legends of treasure and spirits flow from this this fairy tale-like oasis.

name: The Skull Tower of Nis
description: Structure built from the skulls of vanquished foes.

name: Golubac Fortress
description: This 14th century castle withstood years of battle but is now sliding off the hills into the Danube.

name: Belgrade Tesla Museum
description: Nikola Tesla's ashes are held in a golden sphere, surrounded by his works.

name: Uvac River Meanders
description: A sinuous path among steep cliffs creates a stunning sight in southwestern Serbia.

name: Ružica Church
description: A Serbian church lit by chandeliers made of weapons.

name: Bubanj Memorial Park
description: This memorial park stands on the site of one of the largest mass executions of the Second World War.

name: Western City Gate of Belgrade
description: A pair of connected skyscrapers form this symbolic gateway.

name: Ruins of Yugoslav Army Headquarters
description: Destroyed Serbian army headquarters sits half crumbled in a largely revitalized Belgrade.

name: Kosmaj Monument
description: This spaceship-like Serbian monument reaches for the stars in celebration of the fallen fighters of fascism.

name: Tito's Blue Train
description: Ride in 1960s style on the private luxury train Marshal Josip Tito used to travel around Yugoslavia.

name: Drina River House
description: For over 40 years, this quaint little house has teetered on a rock in the middle of a river.

name: Gavez Klub
description: Tucked in an urban forest, this restaurant specializes in homemade, healing rakijas.

name: St. George's Church
description: The interior of this Serbian royal family's mausoleum is covered with exquisite mosaics.

name: Devil's Town Rock Formation
description: The bizarre rocks of Đavolja Varoš are the result of natural erosion.

name: Maglič
description: The ruins of this medieval castle on a foggy hill tell the story of a once-mighty empire.

name: 'Catacombs' of Petrovaradin Fortress
description: A maze of underground tunnels beneath one of the most beautiful forts in Serbia.

name: Hermitage of Saint Sava
description: This mesmerizing medieval monastery-cave complex sits thousands of feet above ground overlooking a valley.

name: Subotica's Art Nouveau Synagogue
description: Religious perseverance and cultural coexistence manifest in an architectural gem that multiple wars couldn't topple.

name: Stopića Cave
description: Through the colossal entrance to this cave you'll find limestone tubs and an underground waterfall.

name: Museum of Yugoslavia
description: A museum dedicated to the former country.

name: Red Cross Concentration Camp
description: A Red Cross memorial museum at the site of the Niš concentration camp that operated for four eventful years during World War II.

name: Kadinjača Memorial Complex
description: A grand Yugoslav memorial to the group of partisans who fought and died resisting western Serbia’s Nazi occupation.

name: Crna Reka Monastery
description: This fresco-covered medieval monastery hangs from the side of a steep cliff.

name: Poslastičarnica sa stavom
description: This ice cream parlor serves scoops of flavors like rakia, medlar, and poppy seed.

name: Arača
description: Despite being pillaged and destroyed a number over times down the centuries, this historic church still sort of stands.

name: Bač Fortress
description: A well preserved medieval fortress in Vojvodina.

name: Spitzer Castle
description: A beautiful 19th-century mansion slowly crumbled and became a 'ghost house' after World War II.

name: Rosomača Canyon
description: The ridged layers that make up this river canyon represent Earth's geological history.

name: Veliki Buk Waterfall
description: A hidden stream deep in the mountains creates one of the largest tufa waterfalls in Serbia.

name: Belgrade Aviation Museum
description: In the hinterlands of Serbia's capital, a giant reflective mushroom houses an aeronautical history of the former Yugoslav republic.

name: Popina Memorial Park
description: Serbia's master of striking memorials erected a forgotten, hidden gem in the hills outside a tiny village.

name: 'Roman Well' of the Belgrade Fortress
description: A misnamed well, with a deep dark past.

name: Vratna Gates
description: These natural stone bridges are the largest on the European continent.

name: World’s Only Art Nouveau McDonald’s
description: Housed in a 1912 architectural masterpiece, this outlet of the Golden Arches is a real beauty.

name: Manasija Monastery
description: This 15th-century Serbian Orthodox monastery also contained a school for illuminating manuscripts.

name: Tabula Traiana
description: A Roman monument to their own civil engineering prowess.

name: Drvengrad
description: An idyllic traditional timber village on a picturesque hill was built for a film.

name: Šalinac Grove
description: An eerie, protected forest of old-growth oak trees, many of which are now dead.

name: Šumarice Memorial Park
description: Abstract monuments express sorrow and resilience at the site of a horrific massacre in Nazi-occupied Serbia.

name: Stratište Memorial Complex
description: An abandoned monument remembering the "Pančevo Holocaust" now stands as a shadow of its former self.

name: Sopotnički Vodopadi
description: This natural monument has an origin story steeped in legend.

name: Vucjanka River
description: Popular river that runs through the remote Serbian municipality of Leskovac.

name: Rajac Cemetery
description: Beautifully carved tombstones fill this unmarked graveyard, uniquely situated near the village's famous stone wine cellars.

name: Church of the Holy Apostles Peter and Paul
description: The oldest church in Serbia was also the ecclesiastical seat of the region during the Middle Ages.

name: Raichle's Palace
description: This dazzling Art Nouveau mansion is now home to the Modern Art Gallery Subotica.

name: Slobodište Memorial Park
description: Curious carved stones and bird-like wings are a symbol of Serbian resilience and freedom on the site of a WWII massacre.

name: Čačak Mausoleum of Struggle and Victory
description: This symbol of Yugoslav liberation decorated with 620 carved heads of mythical creatures is now a forlorn relic of another time.

name: Monastery Blagoveštenje Gornjačko
description: This outcropping was once home to a medieval monastery destroyed during the Ottoman conquest.

name: Monument to the U.S. Bombing of the Chinese Embassy in Belgrade
description: A small memorial to a deadly night in Serbia that shook the world before it was swept under the rug.

name: Pačir Geothermal Spa
description: This natural spa is known for its healing properties and vibrant hue.

name: Lepenski Vir
description: Stone age urban planning.

name: Staro Selo Open-Air Museum
description: This historical village takes you back to the 19th century.

name: Eastern City Gate of Belgrade
description: This feat of Brutalist architecture is also known as the Three Sisters.

name: Bald Jesus Fresco
description: This fresco inside a Serbian cave church has been a source of disagreement.

name: Gradac Monastery
description: This medieval monastery represents an architectural blend of eastern and western European culture.

name: Pinova Vila
description: Once a beautiful palace, this structure is now known as the ''museum of destruction."

name: Ečka Kula
description: Ruins of an old water tower that look like a horror-film castle.

name: Ruins of Felix Romuliana at Gamzigrad
description: Imperial palace of Emperor Galerius, the Emperor behind the worst Christian persecutions during the Roman Empire.

name: Monument to the Unknown Hero
description: Near Belgrade, an ancient-looking sarcophagus serves as a World War I monument.

name: Zemun Fortress
description: Different sections of the Zemun Fortress were built in the 14th and 15th century as a response to the advance of the Ottoman Empire.

name: Hero Dog Monument
description: Dedicated to a brave pooch who fought a tiger to save a young child.

name: Stjepan Filipović Monument
description: A tribute to a man who defied the Nazis until his last breath.

name: ‘Beli Anđeo’ (‘White Angel’)
description: After this medieval Serbian fresco was hidden for centuries, an image of it became one of the first to be sent into space.

name: Latin Church
description: An 11th-century Byzantine church with medieval Serbian history and spectacular views.

name: Viminacium
description: At this archaeological site, visitors can explore the ruins of the capital of the former Roman province of Moesia.

name: Ada Stonehenge
description: This art installation pays homage to the famed stones of European lore.

name: Bridge of Love
description: Sealing couples' love since just after World War I.

name: Igrali Se Konji Vrani (Black Horses at Play)
description: The unusual sculptures are believed to be powerful political symbols.

name: Monument to the Victims of the Sajmište Concentration Camp
description: Memorial to the victims of the Holocaust in Serbia.

name: Pobednik (The Victor) Monument
description: A towering spire in the fortress of Kalemegdan honors the victorious armies of Serbia during their struggles against the Ottoman Empire.

name: Kisiljevo Cemetery
description: The final resting place of one of the first alleged vampires.

name: Studenica Monastery
description: This beautiful medieval Serbian monastery is home to frescoes, peculiar sculptures, and historical curiosities.

name: 'Sami'
description: One of Belgrade's most beloved animals.

name: Vespa Muzej Srbija (Vespa Museum Serbia)
description: A museum dedicated to the classic Italian scooter in the heart of Serbia.

name: Park Maketa
description: A little theme park full of miniature monasteries and churches, made by hand.

name: Pijani Sat (Drunk Clock)
description: Backwards hour and minute hands and a timing mechanism that fluctuates with the temperature led to this clock tower's local nickname.

name: 'Evropska Unija'
description: This statue of a goat in boots is meant to represent the European Union.

name: Knežev Arsenal
description: The ruins of a military-industrial complex now host concerts and more.

name: Raša Popov Monument
description: This statue of the famed author, actor, and journalist is made entirely of screw-nuts.

name: Icarus on the Air Force Command Building
description: An Art Deco Icarus statue adorns this abandoned gem of Yugoslav Modernist architecture.

name: Monastery of the Holy Mother of God, Ston
description: This former monastery is still a revered religious site.

name: Televizorke
description: Two buildings named after their windows that make it look like a sea of television sets plastered to a wall.

name: Hotel Moskva
description: This striking, historic hotel has major Wes Anderson movie energy.

name: 'Muzikanti'
description: These unique sculptures form a musical ensemble of recycled metal.

name: Spomenik Šljivi
description: A Serbian monument to plums.

name: Znak Pitanja (?)
description: Belgrade’s oldest restaurant has a rather unusual name.

name: Serbia's Rocky Statue
description: It may seem a little out of place in a tiny village, but the inspiring story of its creation made headlines.

name: Muzej Šibica
description: A Museum with a collection of 40,000 matchboxes.

name: Bob Marley Monument
description: The first European statue dedicated to the legendary musician.

name: Srpski Holywood
description: A Hollywood-sign replica with a typo on a remote hillside.

name: Most Na Suvom
description: Once a bridge over the River Begej, now high and dry.

name: Muzej Voštanih Figura (The Wax Museum)
description: A quirky little wax museum is home to some of the most famous Serbians in history.

name: Auto Škola Euro Start
description: Lodged in the wall of this driving school is a Zastava 750, one of the region's most popular cars.

name: Kula Gradina
description: This tower is all that remains from a medieval fortress.

name: Belgrade Milestone
description: This small bronze pyramid marks the center of Belgrade, but some say it is a Masonic symbol.

name: 'Tamiški Kralj' ('King of Tamis')
description: A large concrete replica of a 12th-century figurine.

name: YU Biznis Centar
description: Considered an architectural abomination by some, this metal-clad building is known as 'little red riding hood' by locals.

name: Lapidarium
description: An open-air museum dedicated to tombstones and roadside stone monuments.

name: Kalvarice
description: These stairs feature the names of Zemun's most famous faces.

name: Muzej Žeravica
description: What started as a collection of vintage tractors turned into a museum filled with cars, radios, and other oddities.

name: Leo
description: This monument honors a true hero dog.

name: Vucje Hydroelectric Plant
description: Operating continuously since 1903, this "milestone" facility transformed the regional economy.

name: Muzejska zbirka starovremenskih motocikala (Motorcycle Museum in Pančevo)
description: This museum houses a large collection of old motorcycles.

name: We Were Just Children
description: Memorial to the 89 children killed during the NATO bombing of Serbia.

name: Mangulica & Pulin Statue
description: Statues of two of Srem's most well-known animals.

name: The Why? Memorial
description: A NATO bombing took the lives of 16 civilians in 1999—the ruins remain in the center of Belgrade.

name: Požega Railway Museum
description: This open-air, narrow-gauge railway museum pays homage to the region's transportation history.

name: Sava Centar
description: A retro Yugoslav congress, cultural, and business hall built in the 1970s, with an iconic world clock.

name: Spomenik Sibinjanin Janku
description: This statue of a 15th-century Hungarian military and political figure has become known for its resemblance to a particular cartoon knight.

name: Bukovička Banja Park
description: This spa park features an open-air museum of abstract marble sculptures.

name: Duboki Potok Lake
description: A hidden gem to enjoy nature in its purest form.

name: Spomenik Mramor
description: A neglected spomenik overlooking the beautiful Kopaonik Mountains.

name: Vrabac Gočko
description: This statue became the mascot of Vrnjačka Banja after Games Without Borders made a stop in this town.

name: Staro Zdanje
description: Once one of the most beautiful buildings in Serbia, this palace stands abandoned in a park.

name: Mileševac Fortress
description: This medieval fortress guards a monastery near the border of Serbia and Montenegro.

name: Embassy of France in Belgrade
description: Arguably Belgrade's only example of pure Art Deco architecture.

name: Varoško Groblje
description: Zastava automobile sculptures and images grace various gravestones in this cemetery.

name: Čukur Fountain
description: An eerie fountain commemorating the death of a boy that sparked a small war in the 19th century.

name: Fetislam Fortress
description: One of the most important fortresses along the Danube now lies in ruins.

name: 'Crvena Kap'
description: A sculpture of a blood drop designed to honor those that have donated the precious liquid.

name: Jovanča Micić Monument
description: The figure atop this globe is from a Serbian play about a merchant's travels around the world.

name: Monument of Revolution in Ruma
description: A series of metal trumpets in the heart of Ruma represents the seven Vojvodina People's Liberation Brigades.

name: Hermitage of Saint Sava in Kablar
description: A chapel dedicated to Saint Sava built on the rugged cliffs of Kablar.

name: Dvorine
description: The remains of a luxurious 14th-century church built by the last despot of Serbia.

name: Zastava Industrial Complex
description: This abandoned factory once produced Yugoslavia’s iconic Yugo automobile.

name: Fantast Castle
description: A rich Serb hired foreign craftsmen to secretly build the castle of his dreams.

name: Football Monument
description: Dedicated to one of the pioneers of Serbian football.

name: 20 Cola Museum
description: A unique museum documents pop culture and bicycles from the 20th century Serbia.

name: Smoke Cave
description: A cave-turned-memorial church marking the site where civilians were killed during an 1814 uprising.

name: Spomenik Prvoj Štafeti (Monument to the First Relay)
description: This statue honors the first Relay of Youth.

name: Monument of Revolution in Ivanjica
description: A large open-air Socialist Realist mosaic, considered the largest in Serbia.

name: Krusevac Fortress
description: The medieval fortress of Prince Lazar of Serbia is now an archaeological park.

name: 'Smak'
description: Four monuments dedicated to the legendary band in Serbia.

name: Bishop’s Palace
description: The only Baroque residence for bishops of the Serbian Orthodox Church in the Banat region.

name: The Monument of Stevan Sremac and Kalča
description: A bronze sculpture honoring a famous Serbian writer and a character from his novel.

name: Hotel Yugoslavia
description: One of Serbia's oldest luxury hotels and a nostalgic echo to Yugoslavia in the state's former capital.
